Клиника 1 прайс 2024.docx.estimate_method: Opened the docx with python-docx after accept_revisions(); it has ONE table of 2727 rows x 3 cols (Код | Наименование услуги | Стоимость, тенге). I classified rows: 79 are merged full-width banner rows where col0==col1 (e.g. 'Раздел 1.Консультации специалистов', '12.1 Хирургия общая', 'CHECK-UP'), of which 1 is the document title before the column-header row; plus 1 column-header row ('Код | Наименование услуги | Стоимость'). Counting the remaining rows that carry a numeric value in the cost column yields exactly 2646 priced service lines. Cross-checked by mapping every parser output row back to its source row index: all 2646 real priced rows ARE emitted (0 missing), and the parser ADDITIONALLY emits 78 banner rows (70 with a fake numeric price = the section number).
Клиника 1 прайс 2024.docx.coverage_summary: Coverage of real services is complete: all 2646 priced service lines are emitted, with clean names, correct prices, and codes correctly placed in service_code_source (e.g. 'U1.1', 'KR5.3', '10401', 'B06.415.006'). There are NO missing pages/sections, NO glyph-digit prices, NO leading-index or leading-code glued into names, NO spaced-glyph names, and NO wrong-column selection. The doc has a single resident-price column (no non-resident column), so every price_nonresident=None is legitimate. The ONLY problem is OVER-extraction: 78 merged full-width section/sub-section banner rows are emitted as data rows (70 of them with a fake numeric price derived from the section number), inflating the priced count and polluting results with non-service junk rows.

defects.description: Merged full-width 'Раздел N.' section-header rows (in docx the merged cell expands to 3 identical cells) are emitted as priced service rows. parse_price reads the section number out of the cost cell as the price (Раздел 1. -> 1.0, Раздел 3. -> 3.0, ... Раздел 31. -> 31.0). Root cause: _section_label() in table_extract.py returns None whenever the row has more than one non-empty cell, so a merged banner (3 identical cells) is never recognized as a heading.

fix_recommendations: app/parsers/table_extract.py :: _section_label(): recognize merged full-width banners. Replace the early guard 'if len(nonempty) != 1: return None' with logic that also accepts a merged banner — i.e. compute merged_banner = (len(nonempty) >= 2 and len(set(nonempty)) == 1) and proceed (collapsing to that single text) when either len(nonempty)==1 OR merged_banner. This is what makes docx merged section rows (3 identical cells) detectable; in pdfplumber/xlsx this same condition only triggers on genuinely repeated/merged banner cells, so it is safe for all formats sharing this function.

app/parsers/table_extract.py :: _section_label(): a merged banner is STRUCTURALLY a heading even when it embeds a number, so bypass the value-vs-heading guard for it. Change 'if val is not None and not lead_heading: return None' to 'if val is not None and not lead_heading and not merged_banner: return None', and add merged_banner to the final accept condition ('if lead_heading or merged_banner or upper_ratio >= 0.6 or _SECTION_HINT_RE.search(text)'). This drops the numbered sub-section banners ('12.1 Хирургия общая', '31.20.…', '2.2.Выездные…') whose leading number would otherwise be read as a price.

Net effect in rows_from_table(): the 78 banner rows are skipped (continue on section detection) instead of emitted, so the 70 fake-price rows and 8 None-price junk rows disappear, leaving exactly the 2646 real priced services; current_section now flows the banner text onto following rows via extra['section'], restoring specialty/section grouping.

Guard against over-stripping: keep the existing letters>=3 check so a bare numeric/merged cell with no real text is not labeled a section, and do NOT add any leading-digit name-stripping — real lab names legitimately start with digits (17-ОН прогестерон, 25-ОН витамин Д, 11-деоксикортизол, '1 точечный тейп'); the codes are already isolated in service_code_source so no name-side index/code stripping is needed for this file.

Add a regression fixture for this file asserting: row count >= 2640 (expected 2646), every must_appear_rows name+res present exactly, no emitted name matches forbidden patterns '^Раздел\b' or '^\d+\.\d', and price_nonresident is None for all rows (single-column price list).

Клиника 1 2026.pdf.estimate_method: 85-page born-digital PDF with NO ruled tables, so pdfplumber.extract_tables returns nothing and the parser uses the word-geometry path (_rows_from_pdf_words). Anchor: counted space-grouped thousands price tokens (e.g. '16 600', '149 700', '2 400') with value 1000-400000 across all pages = 2674; each priced service line carries exactly ONE price (single 'Стоимость, тенге (2026 год)' column, no non-resident column, so every nonres is null). That misses (a) glyph-corrupted prices that are not pure digits ('21 ООО', 'И 600', '6 90 С', '3 ООО') and (b) ungrouped 3-digit prices ('Холодовая проба 600', 'Забор крови с вены 800') which both ADD real rows, so true count is somewhat higher -> ~2750. Cross-check: 2127 same-line name+price lines + 689 standalone price-only (wrapped) lines = ~2816 price-bearing lines, consistent with ~2750 after removing a little page furniture. Code-token counting is unreliable here (codes are heavily OCR-mangled: 'и 1.2','ОТ7.Ю','МОЗО.47','01121').
Клиника 1 2026.pdf.coverage_summary: Severe under-extraction: 1441 of ~2750 priced lines (~52%) are emitted. No whole specialty section is missing (all 31 'Раздел' headers, pages 1-77, are visited; page 78 is genuinely blank), but EVERY section is heavily under-extracted. Three structural causes: (1) the service code is rendered on its own vertical baseline, so pdfplumber's _pdf_lines splits it onto a separate one-token line — the code is then lost entirely AND single-token code lines plus wrapped name/price lines never form rows (~550 rows lost); (2) _row_from_line treats ANY cell that merely contains a digit as a price, so any service name with an embedded number is consumed and the row is dropped with an empty name (686 rows lost); (3) on the ~74 repeated 'Приложение № 1' page footers the line 'медицинских услуг' is mis-detected as a section header and clobbers the real 'Раздел N' for the rest of that page. The parser is NOT over-extracting (no price=1.0 section-rows leaked), but among the rows it DOES keep, ~775 carry a glued specialty code, ~45+ carry a glued numeric lab code, and dozens have glyph-corrupted prices.

defects.description: _row_from_line (app/parsers/pdf_text.py) walks cells right-to-left calling parse_price; parse_price returns a value for ANY string containing a digit, so a service-name cell with an embedded number (e.g. '...до 60 минут', '1 год', '25-ОН', '0,5 см') is consumed as a price, cut reaches 0, name becomes empty and the row returns None and is dropped. Affects same-line name+price rows whose name embeds a digit, and almost all lab rows whose name starts with a digit.

fix_recommendations: app/parsers/pdf_text.py :: _row_from_line — STOP treating any digit-containing cell as a price. Only consume a trailing cell as a price when it is purely price-like, e.g. re.fullmatch(r'[\d\s.,]+', cell) (after glyph normalization), NOT when the cell contains alphabetic characters. Also hard-guard: never return a row whose name is empty. This single change recovers the 686 embedded-digit drops and stops names being eaten. Highest priority.

app/parsers/pdf_text.py :: _rows_from_pdf_words / _pdf_lines — stitch the code-only line and wrapped name/price fragments back into one logical row. The service code is rendered on its own y-baseline; cluster lines into row-bands (or merge a line that is just a code token, and a standalone price-only line like '3 900', onto the adjacent name line) before calling _row_from_line. Recovers ~550 wrapped rows.

Add a shared leading-code extractor (new helper, call it from _row_from_line and from table_extract.rows_from_table) that moves a leading code token into service_code_source and strips it from the name. Match (a) specialty/OCR codes '^[A-Za-zА-Яа-яЁё]{1,3}\s?\d{1,2}(\.\d{1,2})+' (U1.5, KR5.1, ОТ7.66, 'и 1.2') and (b) pure/dotted numeric lab codes '^\d{3,}(\.\d+)*\b' / '^\d{1,3}(\.\d{1,3})+\b' (1151, 10401, 40.080, B06.127.006). CRITICAL SAFETY: only strip when the token is followed by whitespace+letter, and NEVER strip leading digits immediately followed by '-', ',', 'ОН', 'OH' (protect '25-ОН витамин Д', '17-ОН', '1,25-', '10,11-дигидро', 'ВИЧ 1,2').

app/parsers/table_extract.py :: parse_price (or a pre-pass) — add glyph-digit normalization for PRICE cells only: map look-alikes О/о/o/O->0, С/с/c->0, З/з->3 inside a numeric context, and handle the 'И'->'11' leading case ('И 600'->'11 600'). Re-parse after substitution. Guard so clean numeric cells are untouched. Fixes 'Прием врача трихолога' 600->11600, '21 ООО'->21000, '3 ООО'->3000, '6 90 С'->6900.

app/parsers/pdf_text.py :: _cells_by_gap / _row_from_line — do not split space-grouped thousands ('6 320','6 900') and tighten the qty-strip 'while prices[0][0] < 10 <= prices[1][0]'. Treat a 1-digit token immediately left of a 3-digit token within a normal inter-word space as one price; only drop a leading small integer when it is a genuinely separate wide-gap column. Fixes 'Корь IgG' 320->6320.

app/parsers/table_extract.py :: _section_label — reject page-furniture as section headers. Add a blacklist / require Раздел|Категория-style lead OR uppercase-dominant AND not matching repeated header/footer fragments ('медицинских услуг', 'к договору на оказание', 'Приложение', dates). Prevents 'медицинских услуг' from clobbering 'Раздел N' on the ~74 footer pages. NOTE: this file is shared by xlsx/docx/ocr parsers — guard the change so legitimate section rows in other formats still pass.

app/parsers/table_extract.py :: _section_label — optionally normalize section digit glyphs ('Раздел З.'->'Раздел 3.', 'Раздел Ю.'->'Раздел 10.', 'Раздел 8,'->'Раздел 8.', 'Раздел 19.0 нкология'->'Раздел 19.Онкология') for consistent grouping.

app/parsers/pdf_text.py :: extract_header_hints / _first_partner_line — exclude column-header tokens ('Стоимость', 'Наименование', 'Код', '(20NN год)', 'тенге') so partner_name_hint isn't 'Стоимость,' and city_hint isn't 'од'; leave hints None when only column furniture is present.

Клиника 2 прайс 2025 год.PDF.estimate_method: The № column numbers priced services sequentially and continuously from 1 to a maximum of 1076 (last line: '1076 Пакет услуг №2: артромот+ЛФК+реабилитационное кресло+ручная разработка 1 процедура 12 000'). Section/category headers use an INDEPENDENT 1..44 numbering ('8 Гинекология', '40 Онкология, маммология', etc.) plus 3 block headers ('Блок А/В/С'), and carry no unit/price, so they are excluded. An independent heuristic — counting lines that begin with an index AND contain a unit keyword (посещение/услуга/процедура/исследование/операция/пакет/единица/...) — found 1040 single-line priced rows; adding the ~30-40 rows whose descriptive name wraps so the index lands on a separate visual line lands at ~1076, matching the max sequential number. A few numbers may be skipped/duplicated, but 1076 is the best estimate.
Клиника 2 прайс 2025 год.PDF.coverage_summary: Severe under-extraction: the parser emits only 142 of ~1076 priced rows (~13%). Pages 1-3 are partially captured (32/19/34 rows), but coverage collapses after page 3 — pages 4-23 yield only 0-9 rows each, and pages 6, 16, and 23 yield ZERO rows. The collapse tracks the right-margin glyph corruption that grows on later pages: when a price's trailing '0' is rendered as a lookalike glyph (Cyrillic С/О or '(') the rightmost cell is non-numeric, so _row_from_line breaks with no price and DROPS the whole row. Multi-line wrapped services (name on its own visual line, with №+unit+price on another) are also dropped or misread as section headers. There is minor over-extraction noise (wrapped service-name fragments leak into the 'section' field) but the dominant problem is the ~87% of rows missing entirely.

defects.description: Whole pages/sections are dropped. The parser captures 142 of ~1076 priced rows; pages 4-23 yield only 0-9 rows each and pages 6/16/23 yield zero. Root cause is in app/parsers/pdf_text.py _row_from_line(): it walks cells right-to-left and breaks (returning None, dropping the entire row) the moment the rightmost cell fails to parse as a price>0 — which happens whenever the price's last digit is a lookalike glyph (С/О/'(') or the name wraps across lines.

E.g. '10 80С'->10800, '24 ООС'->24000, '60('->600, '108 ООО'->108000, '144 00С'->144000.

fix_recommendations: 1. app/parsers/table_extract.py parse_price(): add a glyph-normalization pass BEFORE _NUMBER_RE. In a helper _normalize_price_glyphs(token), when a whitespace-separated token is composed only of digits + known lookalikes, map Cyrillic О/о(U+041E/U+043E)->0, Cyrillic С/с(U+0421/U+0441)->0, '('->0, Latin O/o->0, Latin I/l->1, Cyrillic З->3. Apply only to price-candidate tokens (mostly digits) so service NAMES are never altered. This recovers truncated values like '10 80С'->10800, '24 ООС'->24000, '60('->600.

2. app/parsers/pdf_text.py _row_from_line(): stop dropping rows whose rightmost cell is a non-numeric glyph. First glyph-normalize trailing cells, then scan trailing cells for the price instead of breaking on the first non-numeric token. This is the single change that recovers the ~900 dropped rows on pages 4-23.

3. app/parsers/pdf_text.py _rows_from_pdf_words(): make the running № index the row anchor. Detect lines whose first token is the next sequential index; a line that has no index and no price is a NAME continuation to be appended to the adjacent indexed row (handles 2-3 line wraps like service 3 'отоларинголог' and service 36 'Транспортировка'). Two-pass: collect indexed rows, then attach orphan name lines above/below.

4. app/parsers/pdf_text.py _row_from_line() (after name is built): strip the leading row-index with re.sub(r'^\d{1,4}\s+', '', name) GUARDED so it only fires when the stripped integer equals the expected running index — preserving legitimate digit-leading names ('3-х дневное лечение несовершенного остеогенеза' idx 833; '1 койка без питания' idx 989). Also detect and exclude the unit column ('1 посещение'/'I посещение' normalized) so units are not glued into the name.

5. app/parsers/table_extract.py _section_label() (and the PDF section path in _rows_from_pdf_words): tighten section detection so wrapped service-name lines are NOT treated as headers. Only treat as a section: the block lines ('Блок ...') and SHORT lines with a small leading number, no unit keyword, and no price (the numbered category headers '8 Гинекология','40 Онкология, маммология'). Remove reliance on _SECTION_HINT_RE matching ordinary service words ('прием','консультац','услуг','исследован') for PDF section detection.

6. (lower priority) Handle right-margin digit clipping ('192 00'->192000): after parsing, flag prices that are implausibly ~10x below neighbouring rows in the same section / not aligned to the column's typical granularity, rather than silently emitting the truncated value.

Those 1-846 numbered lines are the canonical priced services. Add ~8-10 un-numbered priced sub-rows that carry their own prices (the "Медикаментозный аборт" component breakdowns on p.5 — медикаментозное средство / ОАК / группа крови / прием гинеколога / УЗИ — and the "Выезд врача на дом" components). Клиника 2 прайс 2026.pdf.coverage_summary: All 15 pages are read and the raw row COUNT (847) coincidentally lands near the true ~850, so NO whole page/section is missing — coverage by count is fine. The damage is in content quality, not coverage. This is a poor-quality OCR text layer embedded in a text PDF (pdfplumber finds 0 ruled tables, so the word-geometry path `_rows_from_pdf_words` is used). ~96% of names carry a glued leading № and a glued trailing unit token; 55 multi-line services collapse to a bare "№ + unit" with the descriptive name DROPPED entirely (under-extraction of names); sections are ~100% wrong (page furniture "НДС)" assigned to 525 rows) — over-extraction of furniture as sections and total miss of the real numbered specialty headers; and ~15-20 rows get wrong res/nonres because lookalike-glyph digits ("II 000", "II 900", "4 ЗОО") and a stray table-border "1" break price parsing.

Артикаин Гидрохлорид 4%, 1:100 000, Huons Co., Ltd., Корея

NOTE: in THIS document NO real service name STARTS with a digit — every leading digit token is a row index, so '^\d{1,4}\s' stripping is safe. The names listed here only EMBED digits and must be preserved.
fix_recommendations: 1. (pdf_text.py::_row_from_line, and helper in _cells_by_gap) Normalize glyph-digit price cells BEFORE parse_price by reusing app/parsers/ocr_clean.fix_numeric_artifacts on each trailing candidate cell. This turns 'II 000'->'11 000', 'II 900'->'11 900', '4 ЗОО'->'4 300', 'S 600'->'5 600', so the trailing-numeric scan no longer breaks at a glyph cell and res/nonres land in the correct columns. Also extend ocr_clean._looks_numeric / _DIGIT_FIXES to repair ALL-glyph tokens (e.g. 'ЗЗООО'=33000, which currently has no real digit so is skipped) when the cell sits among confirmed price cells.

2. (pdf_text.py::_row_from_line) After building `name`, strip a leading row-index token: re.sub(r'^\s*\d{1,4}\s+(?=[^\d])', '', name). Use the (?=[^\d]) lookahead so a digit-leading lab code (none here, but e.g. '17-ОН...') is never truncated. In this doc no real name starts with a digit, so this is safe.

3. (pdf_text.py::_row_from_line) Treat the Единица-измерения cell as a separate cell and exclude it from the name: when the last non-price cell is a unit token, drop it. Add a _UNIT_VALUES set = {'услуга','услуги','прием','приём','Услуга','сеанс','коэфф'} and pop it from cells[:cut] before joining the name.

4. (pdf_text.py::_rows_from_pdf_words) Add multi-line NAME MERGING: buffer visual lines that are pure text (no trailing price cells, not a section header) and attach them to the adjacent priced row whose name cell is only '№ + unit'. Concretely, carry a pending text fragment from the line above, and also append the immediately-following text-only continuation line, then prepend/append to the priced row's reconstructed name. This recovers the 55 names currently emitted as bare 'NNN услуга'.

5. (table_extract.py::_section_label — shared by the word path via _section_label(cells)) Recognize a numbered specialty header given as TWO cells [<1-3 digit №>, <alphabetic title>] with no parseable price (e.g. ['9','Гинекология'], ['20','Пульмонология'], ['1','Прием и консультации специалиста']) and return the title as the section. Simultaneously add a furniture blocklist so repeated page headers are NEVER taken as a section: reject 'НДС)', titles starting with 'Цена для'/'Цены для', and the fragments 'зарубежье)', '(в том числе', 'Наименование услуг', 'Республики', 'Казахстан'. This fixes both the missed real sections and the 'НДС)'-as-section over-extraction.

6. (pdf_text.py::_row_from_line) Preserve the third price column (страховые / дальнее зарубежье) into extra['price_far_abroad'] when three prices are present, mirroring the near/far tier handling already in table_extract.rows_from_table, so the insurance tier is not silently dropped.

7. (pdf_text.py::_row_from_line) Re-validate the existing leading-tiny-price drop (`while len(prices)>1 and prices[0][0]<10<=prices[1][0]`) after the above changes; with index/unit/glyph fixes in place it should rarely fire, and must not discard a legitimately small first price.

Клиника 3 прайс 2026.PDF.format: pdf (24-page scanned PDF with a corrupted embedded OCR text layer; pdfplumber finds 0 ruled tables, so pdf_text.py reconstructs rows from word geometry via _rows_from_pdf_words/_row_from_line/_cells_by_gap)
Клиника 3 прайс 2026.PDF.estimate_method: Counted, per page, source lines ending in two space-grouped price numbers (the "Цена, тенге" + "Цена со ... партнёра" columns): 723 across all 24 pages. Added the ~40 real services on the structurally-broken pages 1 and 3 whose price/code/material sit on a different visual line from the name (the 2-price heuristic only caught 24 on p1 and 2 on p3, vs ~14 and ~30 real services), plus a handful of single-price rows, and subtracted ~10 header/furniture lines. The document uses multiple restarting index sequences (main lab list ~1–389 across pp.1–17, an allergen-panel block renumbered 1–204 on pp.17–22, and a chromatography/ХМС-hormones block 63–75 on p.24), consistent with ~740–750 priced service lines.
Клиника 3 прайс 2026.PDF.coverage_summary: No whole pages are dropped — all 24 pages emit rows and the total (712) is close to the true ~745. The parser is NOT under-extracting count-wise on the bulk of pages; instead it is corrupting almost every row. On the cleanly-OCR'd majority of pages it maps ~1 row per service but contaminates names with leading row-index tokens (~352 rows, 49%) and/or trailing tariff-code + material tokens, and frequently misreads glyph-garbled price cells or reads tariff codes as prices. Pages 1 and 3 are structurally broken: the service NAME sits on its own line with no price, so it is demoted to a bogus "section" while the material column ('кровь с ЭДТА', 'соскоб с') becomes the row name — both under-extraction (lost names) and over-extraction (garbage rows + ~20 bogus section labels) at once.

fix_recommendations: pdf_text.py::_row_from_line — strip a leading row-index token. After building `cells`, if `cells[0]` is a bare small integer (regex ^[.\s]*\d{1,3}[.'`\s]*$, also tolerating OCR junk like '.40' / "58'" / '4 Г') and >=2 alphabetic cells remain, drop it before composing `name`. Additionally apply a string-level guard on the final name: re.sub(r"^\d{1,3}(?=\s+[А-ЯЁA-Za-zа-яё])", '', name) but ONLY when the digits are not immediately followed by '-', ',' or '.' so legit lab names ('17-ОН', '25-ОН', '11-деоксикортизол', '1,25-...') are preserved. Fixes ~352 rows.

pdf_text.py::_row_from_line — strip trailing tariff-code and material tokens from the name and capture the code into ParsedRow.service_code_source. After determining `cut`, pop name-tail tokens matching the tariff-code shape (?:В0[1-9]|ВОЗ|Q9\d|СН)\.\d{2,3} and any token in a material set {сыв., моча, кал, плазма, слюна, кровь с ЭДТА, сыворотка, капиллярная кровь, мазки, соскоб, кол.}. Keep allergen-style codes like 'f93'/'gx4' that are part of the name (the tariff regex does not match them).

table_extract.py::parse_price (or a new is_price_cell guard used by _row_from_line's right-scan) — reject a cell as a price when it is not essentially numeric. parse_price('СН.001.148')→1148, parse_price('В02.061.002')→2061002, parse_price('ВОЗ.401.002')→401002 currently corrupt prices. Require a price cell to match ^[\d\s.,]+(?:тг|₸|тенге)?$ after glyph-normalization, and reject cells that contain Cyrillic/Latin letters or a leading code prefix. Kills the 25 res>=100000 rows and the 1148/1151/1151.2 cases.

Add glyph-digit price normalization (extend app/parsers/ocr_clean.py and call it from _row_from_line before parse_price) — within a cell that is predominantly digits+separators, map lookalikes to digits ('“'/'”'/'"'/'~'/'*' → space/0/8 context-dependent; 'O'/'О'→0, 'l'/'I'/'|'→1, 'S'/'З'→3, 'З'→3) so '1 “80'/'1 "80'/'1 ~SO'/'I 7*0' normalize to 1780. Crucially, do NOT silently delete an isolated 1.0 between two 4-digit prices in the leading-qty-drop at pdf_text.py:206 — treat it as a corrupted price to be re-read, not a quantity column.

pdf_text.py::_rows_from_pdf_words — handle the page-1/page-3 structural break where the name is on a separate visual line from the price/code/material line. Buffer text-only (price-less) lines that fall in the name column band and attach them to the adjacent priced line instead of dropping them or feeding them to _section_label. This recovers ~40 lost services and stops 'кровь с ЭДТА'/'соскоб с' from being emitted as names and 'Общий анализ крови (ОАК без СОЭ)' from being emitted as a section.

table_extract.py::_section_label — tighten so wrapped NAME fragments are not misclassified as section headers. The mixed-case keyword acceptance via _SECTION_HINT_RE ('анализ'/'исследован'/'консультац') matches continuation fragments like 'сыворотке крови на анализаторе)'. Require single-cell section rows to be uppercase-dominant (>=0.6 upper) OR match _SECTION_LEAD_RE; reject candidates that start lowercase or end with ')'. Removes the ~20 bogus sections.

pdf_text.py / detect.py — add a parse-quality gate: when a large fraction of extracted rows show glyph-garbage symptoms (prices equal to tariff codes, material-as-name, names with leading indices), the embedded text layer is unreliable; fall back to pdf_scan (re-OCR) or vision_fallback (currently disabled here). Highest leverage but largest change; the per-row fixes above are the immediate, low-risk wins shared across all formats via table_extract.py.

Клиника 5 прайс 2025.pdf.estimate_method: Counted price-bearing money tokens across all 15 pages: a (glyph-)digit run immediately before тг/тенге, after normalizing lookalike glyphs (Cyrillic О/о→0, З→3, б→6, l→1). That yields 367 plausible price tokens (value >=100). Subtracted ~22 second-prices that belong to two-price doctor consultation lines (one service shown with перв./повт. приём, e.g. 'терапевт 9000 тг 7000 тг') to avoid double-counting. Two-column lab/san-book lines that carry two DIFFERENT services on one physical line were left counted as two (correct). Net distinct priced services ≈ 345 (±~20). This squares with a dense 15-page list: ~240 consultation/procedure/UZI lines (pages 1-10), ~105 lab tests (pages 11-14), ~17 physiotherapy/other (pages 14-15).
Клиника 5 прайс 2025.pdf.coverage_summary: No page is skipped at the file level (all 15 pages are read via the word-geometry borderless path; pdfplumber finds zero ruled tables on every page). But the parser severely UNDER-extracts: ~204 of ~345 true priced services (~40% missing). The dominant cause is that this is a poor scanned text layer in a two-column-ish layout where the service NAME and its PRICE frequently sit on separate physical lines; the line-by-line splitter then drops both the name-only line and the price-only line. The entire physiotherapy block (pages 14-15: Электрофорез, ТОКИ Бернара/Дарсонваля, УФО, СВЧ, УВЧ, Ультразвук, Фонофорез, Лазеротерапия, Магнитотерапия, Ингаляция, Массажная кровать, Пульмонолог, Онколог/Маммолог, ФГДС/хеликобактер) is almost entirely lost. Many UZI (pages 9-10) and lab/hormone/oncomarker rows (pages 11-14) survive only as truncated fragment names ('(ГГТП)', 'поджелудочная железа)'). Separately, glyph-only prices vanish and digit-bearing lab names ('антиген СА 125') are dropped. It is NOT over-extracting much; there are some garbage fragment rows but the net is heavy under-extraction.

fix_recommendations: table_extract.py parse_price(): add surgical glyph-digit normalization. Before _NUMBER_RE search, map lookalike glyphs to digits (Cyrillic О/о->0, З->3, б->6, l/I->1, latin O/o->0) ONLY inside price-shaped tokens — i.e. tokens adjacent to a currency unit (тг/тенге/₸/kzt) or tokens that are otherwise all-glyph/space. Do NOT normalize free text, so names like ОАМ, ОАК, СА, Ig, С-пептид, СЗ-комплемента stay intact. This is the shared parser used by xlsx/docx too, so gate strictly on currency-adjacency. Fixes '10 ОООтг'->10000, 'ЗООООтг'->30000, '1 ООО тг'->1000, 'ЗЗООтг'->3300.

pdf_text.py _row_from_line(): stop the trailing-numeric scan from consuming the NAME cell. Only treat a cell as a price when it is price-shaped: its non-numeric residue is empty or just a currency unit/separator (no Cyrillic letters). This prevents 'антиген СА 125 (яичники)' (and СА 19-9, В12, Т3) from having their embedded digits read as the price and the name zeroed; recovers those dropped lab rows.

pdf_text.py _row_from_line(): when a single price-shaped cell contains MULTIPLE numbers (e.g. '9000 тг 7000 тг'), extract all of them and map to res (first) / nonres (second) instead of returning only the first. Fixes lost перв./повт. non-resident prices for doctor consultations.

pdf_text.py _rows_from_pdf_words(): add line-continuation/merge for this borderless two-column layout — (a) buffer a visual line that has a name but NO price and prepend it to the next price-bearing line's name; (b) attach a price-only line ('2500 тг','10 000') to the buffered preceding name. Highest-value coverage fix: recovers ~100+ rows including the whole physiotherapy block (pages 14-15), page-1 'Холтер ЭКГ'/'СМ АД', and the many split lab/hormone names ('гаммаглютамилтрансфераза (ГГТП)', 'антиген СА 19-9 (желудок, поджелудочная железа)').

pdf_text.py _cells_by_gap()/_rows_from_pdf_words(): detect the genuine two-physical-column blocks (san-book page 2, lab pages) by clustering word x0 positions, and split each visual line into left/right (service+price) groups instead of merging them into one name with embedded 'тг'. Validate output against the forbidden \bтг\b-in-name regex.

table_extract.py _section_label() (used by _rows_from_pdf_words via _SECTION_HINT_RE): tighten so split fragments are not mistaken for section headers — reject one-cell candidates that end in a hyphen ('Прием врача-') or that are immediately followed by a continuation line that yields a price; this stops bogus sections ('Прием врача','врач-УЗИ','низкоэнергетичная лазерная терапия') and the truncation of names like 'Прием врача-физиотерапевта'.

pdf_text.py _row_from_line(): strengthen the unit/quantity guard so an embedded unit phrase ('1 час','1 сеанс','1 инъекция','1 уровень') is never taken as price=1.0; instead defer to next-line merge to fetch the real price. Eliminates spurious res/nonres=1.0 ('мочевой пузырь', 'инъекция парабульбарно', 'упражнение на аппарате').

Клиника 4 прайс 2026.pdf.format: pdf (text-layer / borderless whitespace-column PDF, 31 pages, 3 citizen-tier price columns: граждане РК / СНГ / дальнее зарубежье; parsed via pdfplumber word-geometry path in pdf_text.py, used_ocr=False)
Клиника 4 прайс 2026.pdf.estimate_method: Counted price-triples (three space-grouped numeric tokens in sequence) across all 31 pages of pdfplumber text() AFTER normalizing lookalike-glyph digits (Cyrillic О/о→0, И/и→11): 428 triples. Each priced service has exactly one 3-tier price triple (РК / СНГ / дальнее зарубежье), so triples ≈ true priced rows. Cross-checks: a stricter extract_text >=3-group filter gives 340 (under-counts because pages 23-30 fragment their prices and page-3 names are split onto page 4, plus the '13-800' hyphen / 'ООО' glyph rows escape the filter); the current word-geometry parser emits 444 (slightly inflated by garbage/duplicate-split rows). 428 is the best central estimate; true value is ~420-435.
Клиника 4 прайс 2026.pdf.coverage_summary: Count-wise the parser is roughly complete: 444 emitted vs ~428 true priced lines, and every one of the 31 pages yields rows (no blank page). The failure is QUALITY, not coverage. Three structural coverage gaps exist: (1) a cross-page table split — page 3 holds only the NAMES of rows 12-32 of подраздел 1.2 while page 4 holds only their unit+price columns, so ~21 service names are dropped and replaced by unit-only names ('операция','манипуляция','процедура'); (2) Раздел 7 ('7. Бактериологическая лаборатория') is never recognized as a section because it lacks the 'Раздел' prefix and its leading '7.' is read as a value; (3) the third citizen tier (дальнее зарубежье / col 3) is silently discarded on every row by the word-geometry path. The parser is NOT under-extracting row count — it is massively mis-naming rows and corrupting prices.

fix_recommendations: table_extract.parse_price (shared by ALL formats): before numeric parsing, repair price-shaped glyph tokens — replace Cyrillic О/о (U+041E/U+043E)→'0' and И/и (U+0418/U+0438)→'11', and treat a hyphen between digit groups ('13-800')→thousands separator. Gate strictly on price-shape (a digit group immediately followed by a glyph-run of О's, or 'И' followed by a 3-digit group) so real trailing letters like 'антистрептолизина О' are untouched. This alone fixes res=12→12000, 77→77000, 198→198000, 13→13800, 11400, etc.

pdf_text.py _row_from_line: strip exactly ONE leading row-index token from the assembled name. Apply only when name matches ^\d{1,3}\s+ AND the next char is not a hyphen/comma/digit (protects '8-ми','1,25','(1 сустав)'); strip just one integer (so '23 8-ми образная' → '8-ми образная', never '8'). Also handle a leading punctuation+index ('; 32 ...', '1 30 ...').

pdf_text.py _row_from_line / _cells_by_gap: detect the unit-of-measurement cell (the single token just before the trailing price block) against a shared unit lexicon (операция, манипуляция, процедура, исследование, посещение, пакет, видеозвонок, час, сеанс, койко-день, сутки, зона, анализ, снимок, зуб) and move it into extra['unit'] instead of leaving it in the name.

table_extract._section_label (used by both pdf_text and rows_from_table): (a) explicitly reject the bare column headers 'Наименование услуг', 'Единица измерения', and the tier-header phrases so they can never become a section; (b) require a single-cell line to match _SECTION_LEAD_RE or be uppercase-dominant to qualify — do NOT promote ordinary service-name lines (e.g. 'Консультация врача (дмн, профессора)', 'Рентгенография височных костей ...') to sections; (c) extend heading detection to bare-numbered headings like '7. Бактериологическая лаборатория' (pattern ^\d{1,2}\.\s+\p{L} on a price-less line) so Раздел 7 is captured.

pdf_text.py _rows_from_pdf_words: implement wrapped/merged-name recovery — when a priced line's name (after index+unit strip) is empty or just a unit, compose the name from the nearest price-less text line(s) immediately above (and the continuation line below), instead of dropping them or emitting them as sections. Fixes first/repeat consultation pairs, '6 операция', '33 процедура' (Удаление спицы...), etc.

pdf_text.py PdfTextParser.parse: handle the cross-page table split — when page N word-geometry yields names with no price columns and page N+1 yields unit+price columns with no names, zip page-N left-column names onto page-N+1 rows by order (heuristic-gated so normal pages are unaffected). fix_recommendations: 1) app/parsers/table_extract.py :: _section_label(): recognize single-non-empty-cell rows that begin with a hierarchical section number ('1.', '1.1', '15.16', '8.1', '23.') as headings. Before the parse_price value-rejection, strip a leading section-number token with re.sub(r"^\s*\d+(?:\.\d+)*[.)]?\s+", "", text) and run the price/heading test on the de-numbered core; treat the row as a heading when the core has >=3 letters and no parseable price. This is safe because the function already requires exactly ONE non-empty cell and a genuine priced service is never single-cell (it always has name + at least one price cell). This single change removes the 89 junk header rows AND populates extra['section'] for all 5026 real rows (fixes the sec=None defect).

2) Preserve the existing protection for OTHER formats sharing this function: keep the price-on-core check so a merged 'name price' cell (e.g. a PDF row 'Прием врача 5000') still detects a price on its core and is NOT swallowed as a heading. Re-run pdf_text / pdf_scan / docx fixtures after the change.

3) app/parsers/table_extract.py :: rows_from_table(): after computing `name`, drop placeholder rows by adding a small skip-set check, e.g. if name.strip().lower() in {'исключение услуги'}: continue. Removes the 25 deleted-service placeholders.

4) Do NOT add any leading-index/leading-digit stripping to names for this file: № п/п is already a separate column, and many real names legitimately start with or embed digits ('22.1.1 Наблюдение... 7-16 недель', '3.5 ЭКО', '1) Медосмотр...', '25-OH', 'СА 19-9', 'CYFRA 21-1', '17- оксипрогестерон'). Any such fix would corrupt them.

5) Column mapping is already correct and needs no change, but document it: resident=col F 'Цена с учетом НДС для граждан РК', non-resident=col I 'дальнее зарубежье', near-abroad (col H, СНГ) preserved in extra['price_near_abroad']; col E 'без НДС' is intentionally ignored. Verified against rows 13/53/736/5165.

6) Downstream only (not table_extract): when filtering 'priced' rows for display/catalog, treat price==0.0 as a valid price so the 15 free services (e.g. 'Анализ крови на ВИЧ') are not hidden by a truthiness check.

7) Add regression fixtures: assert the 8 must_appear_rows (exact name + res/nonres) are produced, assert none of the forbidden_name_patterns match any emitted name, and assert total emitted rows >= min_rows (5000) and ~5041 after dropping headers+placeholders.

Клиника 7_Прайс 2026.xls.estimate_method: Single sheet '2026' (3065 rows x 6 cols). The source carries an explicit row-number column (col0, '№ п/п'). Exactly 2884 rows bear a numeric index in col0 and the max index value is 2884 (contiguous 1..2884), so there are 2884 real service lines. Of those, 2883 carry a price in at least one of the three price columns (col3 РК / col4 ближнее / col5 дальнее зарубежье); the one exception is the indexed Cito surcharge line (#605, row=690 'Выполнение исследования в ускоренном режиме (по Cito)') which legitimately has empty price cells. 176 additional rows are single-cell section/subsection headers (no index, no price) and ~10 rows are sheet title/furniture above the header row — all excluded from the count.
Клиника 7_Прайс 2026.xls.coverage_summary: Coverage of priced data is essentially complete: the parser emits all 2883 priced service lines plus the 1 priceless indexed Cito line — no whole sheets/pages/sections are missing (there is only one sheet). The problem is OVER-extraction and section corruption, not under-extraction. ~69 mixed-case subsection headers (e.g. 'Витамины', 'Опухолевые маркеры', 'Гормоны щитовидной железы', '- МСКТ - Головной мозг', 'Абдоминопластика') are wrongly emitted as null-price rows AND, more damagingly, are never promoted to the current section, so every priced row beneath them inherits a stale/wrong section label. Title rows above the header ('ПРЕЙСКУРАНТ', 'Приложение к Приказу…') are correctly skipped, and all-caps section headers ('ПРИЕМ ВРАЧА', 'УСЛУГИ ТЕРАПИИ') are handled correctly.

They are therefore emitted as garbage ParsedRows with all prices None. About 69 such rows leak into the output (the 70 None-resident rows minus the one genuine priceless Cito line).

The current gate `if lead_heading or upper_ratio>=0.6 or _SECTION_HINT_RE.search(text)` drops mixed-case subsection headers ('Витамины', 'Опухолевые маркеры', 'Гормоны щитовидной железы', '- МСКТ - Головной мозг', 'Абдоминопластика'). A lone label cell with no price in a priced table is structurally a heading. This single change fixes BOTH the ~69 null-row over-extraction AND the stale-section attribution on the priced rows beneath.

SAFETY GATE — pass a flag from rows_from_table() into _section_label (e.g. `single_cell_is_heading = has_header and ('resident' in mapping or 'nonresident' in mapping)`) and only apply the relaxed branch when the table has a detected header with a real price column. table_extract.py is shared by PDF/DOCX/OCR; without this gate, header-less single-column name lists would be wiped. The 2-non-empty-cell Cito line (row 690, name+unit, no price) is unaffected because the relaxed rule still requires exactly ONE non-empty cell.

DO NOT add leading row-number/index stripping for this format. The № п/п is already a separate mapped 'index' column, and names legitimately begin with digits ('1 ГРУППА СЛОЖНОСТИ', '3Д пазух носа', '1 койко/день…'). defects.description: On sheet 'востребовынные страховых' the detected header is raw row 19 = ['№ п/п','Наименование услуги','Цена (тенге) в том числе НДС', '', ...]. It is a STACKED two-row header: the real sub-column labels (Код по тарификатору / ед.измерения / price-for-citizens) live in raw row 20. _map_columns sees 'Цена' in column index 2 and maps resident->col2, but in the DATA rows col2 holds the MKB-9 service code (e.g. 'A02.004.000.1'). parse_price() therefore turns the code digits into a bogus price, and because res_price is non-None the rightmost-numeric fallback never runs, so the TRUE price in col4 (e.g. 25680) is ignored. Mapping is {index:0, resident:2, name:1}.

fix_recommendations: PRIMARY (app/parsers/table_extract.py, rows_from_table / _map_columns): add a data-validation guard for price columns. After _map_columns, if a column mapped to 'resident' (or 'nonresident' via a 'цена' keyword) has DATA values that are predominantly NON-plain-numeric and instead match a service-code pattern (regex like ^[A-Za-zА-Яа-яЁё]?\d{2}\.\d{3} — catches A02.004.000.1 / B06.157.006 / С03.027.004), reclassify that column as 'code' (if no code column is mapped yet) and DELETE the 'resident'/'nonresident' mapping. The existing rightmost-numeric fallback then correctly picks col4 (25680) into res_price and the code column is recovered. This fixes sheet 'востребовынные страховых' WITHOUT touching sheet 'Страховой' (which already has resident unmapped). Sample data cells to validate against: code col '%-numeric'≈0 vs price col '%-numeric'≈100.

ALTERNATIVE / complementary (table_extract.py, find_header_row + rows_from_table): support stacked multi-row headers — when the row directly below the detected header is itself header-like (no parseable price; contains keywords such as 'код'/'ед.изм'/'для граждан') merge its cell texts column-wise into the header before _map_columns and advance the data-start index. CAUTION: a naive merge here mis-assigns the single price column to 'nonresident' because sheet-2's price sub-header literally contains 'иностранцев' (a _NONRES_KW) and would also regress sheet 1; if you take this route, prefer mapping a sole price tier to 'resident' and only treat it as non-resident when an explicit resident column also exists. The PRIMARY guard above is safer.

SECONDARY (table_extract.py, rows_from_table main loop or _section_label): drop trailing signatory/footer furniture. Require an emitted row to have a price OR a code, OR extend the furniture filter to skip single-cell rows matching signatory titles (^Заместитель директора , ^Главный экономист$, ^Председатель ...). This removes the 6 price-less rows currently emitted (rows 1813-1815 of 'Страховой' and 152-154 of 'востребовынные страховых').

REGRESSION FIXTURE: assert the 7 must_appear_rows (exact name+res+code+section) and the forbidden_name_patterns, and explicitly assert sheet-1 rows (Аллерголог КМН=15480/A02.020.000.2, 17-ОН=3840/B06.573.005) are byte-for-byte unchanged so the shared table_extract.py change is proven non-regressing across the single-header sheet.
